reasoning:
The reference price for this question is the market close on May 23, 2025, which is exactly $73.175. Over the past week (since May 15), GPN stock dropped over 10%, experiencing severe negative momentum. This was primarily driven by market indigestion of major restructuring announcements (a $24B acquisition of Worldpay offset by a $13.5B divestiture of Issuer Solutions) and the accompanying analyst downgrades to the $75–$78 range. 

For the short-term horizon (June 1), predicting 4 trading days out, momentum overwhelmingly dictates market moves; thus, the chance of the sell-off continuing pushes the probability below 0.5. As we look further ahead (June 24, Aug 23), the stock is already trading below all recent bearish price targets (lowest seen is $75), meaning a fundamental floor is likely to establish itself and trigger a mean-reverting bounce. By November 2025, maximizing uncertainty around M&A execution risks versus the intrinsic earnings generation of the new combined company brings our estimate back up precisely to neutral. This perfectly follows the principle of short-term signal dominance decaying toward maximal uncertainty (p=0.50) as the forecast horizon extends.
